lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Overview
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt is a limited-time PvE event mode inside Marvel Rivals, not a standalone game. This overview explains how the mode plays, why the Dracula storyline matters, and what players should focus on first.

steps.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Is an Event Mode
steps.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt runs inside the core game client as an event playlist tied to Season 7.5 and the Dracula invasion storyline.

steps.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Supports Solo and Co-op
steps.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt can be played solo or in a 1-4 player squad, with duplicate hero picks enabled for flexible team setups.

steps.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Uses a PvE Wave Loop
steps.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt centers on wave clears, short upgrade windows, Arcana spending, and boss checkpoints instead of standard PvP rounds.

steps.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Features Six Event Heroes
steps.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt hero pool includes Blade, Jeff the Land Shark, Moon Knight, The Punisher, Squirrel Girl, and Thor.

steps.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Boss Route Scales by Difficulty
steps.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt introduces more boss pressure as difficulty rises, culminating in Dracula progression and hidden Kingpin objectives.

quickTips: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt first clears unlock the fastest reward momentum.
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt runs become smoother when Arcana upgrades are chosen around team roles.
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt hero-specific goals at Nightmare 40 and 70 are key long-term milestones.
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt accessory shards are easiest to optimize when combined with milestone pushes.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Release Date
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt launched on April 23, 2026 at 09:00 UTC and is scheduled to end on July 30, 2026 at 09:00 UTC. Use this date panel to plan clears before the event closes.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Announcement
cards.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt was announced in the official Version 20260423 patch notes published on April 22, 2026.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Launch Time
cards.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt became playable on April 23, 2026 at 09:00 UTC with the Season 7.5 event rollout.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Active Window
cards.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt remains active from April 23, 2026 to July 30, 2026 as a limited-time PvE mode.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt End Date
cards.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt event access closes on July 30, 2026 at 09:00 UTC, so milestone routes should be completed before then.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Time-Zone Planning
cards.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt deadlines are announced in UTC, so convert event cutoffs to local time for squad scheduling.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Deadline Priority
cards.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt high-value targets include first clears, Nightmare milestone rewards, and Kingpin accessory shard progress.

milestones: Before deadline: Finish Nightmare and shard goals

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Use this first-run route to enter Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt, pick a hero, and build momentum from Normal through Nightmare progression.

items.name: Open Playlist and Enter Rivalry
items.description: From the Marvel Rivals main menu, open Playlist, switch to the Rivalry tab, and select the Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt icon.

items.name: Choose a Difficulty for Your First Run
items.description: Start Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt on Normal to learn wave pace and boss timing before moving into Hard and Extreme clears.

items.name: Pick One of Six Event Heroes
items.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt allows duplicate hero picks, so squads can optimize damage, wave control, or survivability.

items.name: Set Gear Slots Before Queueing
items.description: Review weapon, armor, accessory, and exclusive slots so Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt runs start with a stable build baseline.

items.name: Spend Trait Points Between Runs
items.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt scaling improves quickly when trait points are allocated to core damage, defense, and utility needs.

items.name: Use Arcana for Mid-Run Scaling
items.description: In Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt, Arcana choices during runs decide wave stability and boss damage breakpoints.

items.name: Recycle Drops in the Forge
items.description: Convert unwanted drops into Uru Shards so Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt build upgrades stay focused on your main hero route.

items.name: Push Clear Chain: Normal to Nightmare
items.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt progress is strongest when each clear unlocks the next difficulty and milestone reward track.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Rewards
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt rewards are split across first clears, Nightmare milestones, hero-specific challenges, enemy count tracks, and Kingpin shard crafting.

solutions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt First-Clear Rewards
solutions.description: Normal, Hard, and Extreme first clears grant Units, Unstable Molecules, and gallery cards that establish early progression value.

solutions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare Milestones
solutions.description: Nightmare checkpoints reward moods, currencies, titles, and repeated Kingpin Trophy Shards across higher tiers.

solutions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Hero Challenges
solutions.description: Each event hero receives specific Nightmare 40 and Nightmare 70 goals tied to unique emoji and title rewards.

solutions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Enemy Count Rewards
solutions.description: Defeating enemy totals unlocks gallery cards, currencies, spray cosmetics, and the Blood Hunt nameplate.

solutions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Kingpin Trophy Shards
solutions.description: Shard drops from high Nightmare milestones are required for crafting the Kingpin Dethroned Trophy accessory.

solutions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Hidden Boss Bonus
solutions.description: Defeating hidden Kingpin routes unlocks special accessory rewards and additional cosmetic progression.

managementTips: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt first-clear rewards should be completed before deep Nightmare attempts.
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt shard planning is easiest when Nightmare checkpoints are tracked in order.
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt hero challenge titles should be paired with your most stable hero build.
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt enemy-count rewards progress naturally during regular farming sessions.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Kingpin Hidden Boss
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Kingpin is a hidden Phase 14 boss unlocked only when all 3 Golden Keys are completed in one Extreme run before defeating Dracula.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Step 1: Start on Extreme Difficulty
cards.description: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Kingpin route only appears on Extreme, so plan key objectives before Phase 13 instead of treating the run like a normal clear.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Step 2: Phase 4 Arcane Barrier (90s)
cards.description: Sprint to the bridge barrier, break it fast, and enter the checkpoint to secure Golden Key 1 before the 90-second timer expires.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Step 3: Phase 7 Bloodclot Buds
cards.description: Clear Ruins bloodclot buds for Golden Key 2, with scaling counts by team size: Solo 1, Duo 2, Full squad 4.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Step 4: Phase 12 Sealing Circle (120s)
cards.description: Destroy blood crystals around the Castle Entrance within 120 seconds to secure Golden Key 3 and keep the hidden route active.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Step 5: Defeat Dracula to Enter Times Square
cards.description: After all Golden Keys are secured, beating Dracula transitions the run to the Phase 14 Times Square Kingpin encounter.

cards.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Step 6: Kingpin Attack Counters
cards.description: Counter Trance Beam with movement spam and burst, hide from Charged Implosion, sidestep Aerial Slam, and kite Charge Attack at range.

highlights: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt missing any Golden Key ends the route after Dracula
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Phase 4 and Phase 12 timed checks are the two hardest failures
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt teams should call objective spawns immediately to prevent time loss
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Kingpin clear completes hidden objective progression and reward unlocks

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Dracula Boss Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Dracula has four phases built around Bloodstain healing, movement checks, and a final sunlight-powered burst window.

regions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Dracula Phase 1
regions.description: Avoid feeding Bloodstain stacks, then interrupt Dracula's siphon channel with team burst to trigger a short stun and safe damage window.

regions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Dracula Phase 2
regions.description: When Dracula enters blood mist, targeted players must keep moving so the grab misses and opens another punish window.

regions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Dracula Phase 3
regions.description: Track sweeping sword lanes, destroy sentient swords, and preserve revival tools instead of forcing risky damage.

regions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Dracula Phase 4
regions.description: Break both crystals to open the sunlight zone, gain Empowered, then burst Dracula during vulnerability windows.

regions.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Dracula Core Rules
regions.description: Treat Phases 1-3 as survival setup, play mostly at range, and reserve strongest cooldowns for the Phase 4 sunlight sequence.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Best Characters
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt best picks are heroes that control swarms and still deliver boss pressure without relying on perfect team coordination.

creatures.name: Squirrel Girl
creatures.description: Squirrel Girl is a top Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt investment with strong mobility, ranged pressure, and reliable scaling for solo or co-op clears.

creatures.name: Moon Knight
creatures.description: Moon Knight's Phases of the Moon Ankh build gives Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt teams high boss damage, crowd control, and strong reposition tools.

creatures.name: Jeff the Land Shark
creatures.description: Jeff the Land Shark scales hard in Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt once Overflowing Waters and Forked Stream are online for boss burst and wave coverage.

creatures.name: Thor
creatures.description: Thor provides Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt squads with durable frontline stability and reliable damage during revive-heavy runs.

creatures.name: Blade
creatures.description: Blade performs in Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt when built around safer gun-focused pressure, but needs investment and team support at high tiers.

creatures.name: The Punisher
creatures.description: The Punisher adds strong single-target damage in Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt but depends on teammates to handle heavy swarm control.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Builds
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt builds are defined by Traits, Arcana timing, and four gear slots: Weapon, Armor, Accessory, and Exclusive.

items.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Squirrel Girl Ranged Mobility Carry
items.description: Prioritize ranged damage and mobility so Squirrel Girl can kite safely, clear swarms, and still contribute meaningful boss pressure.

items.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Moon Knight Phases of the Moon Build
items.description: Focus on Ankh uptime, ranged weapon scaling, and survivability gear to maximize Moon Knight's endgame boss and wave control value.

items.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Jeff Overflowing Waters Build
items.description: Rush Overflowing Waters and Forked Stream support so Jeff can transition from slower early waves into high-damage boss shredding.

items.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Thor Survival Anchor Build
items.description: Stack durability with steady damage to keep Thor alive during wipe-prone phases and preserve co-op run stability.

items.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Blade Gun-Focused Ranged Build
items.description: Lean into gun-focused traits and sustained ranged pressure because close-range Blade trading is punished heavily in late boss phases.

items.name: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Punisher Single-Target Boss Build
items.description: Build single-target damage and uptime for bosses, while relying on teammates to cover wave control and survival space.

unlockMilestones: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt setup should lock all four gear slots before pushing deep Nightmare
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt high tiers reward ranged pressure and survivability over melee greed
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Arcana spending should align with boss checkpoints and danger waves
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt progression is stronger with one wave-focused and one boss-focused loadout per hero

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare Guide
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.subtitle: Push the Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare ladder from first unlock to Nightmare 120 rewards. Track the boss route, reward milestones, Kingpin Trophy Shards, and solo-clear priorities in one place.
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare is the endgame difficulty path for Blood Hunt. It opens after Normal, Hard, and Extreme progression, then turns the mode into a scaling gauntlet built around Capwolf, Ratatoskr, Dracula, and Kingpin.

items.milestone: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare Unlock
items.requirement: Clear the standard Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Normal, Hard, and Extreme path.
items.focus: Before pushing Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare, concentrate upgrades on one main hero, fill all four Gear slots, and spend Trait Points and Arcana Points instead of spreading progress across every character.
Use the first reward node as a build check. If waves feel slow or unsafe, return to lower tiers for Gear, Trait Points, and Forge materials.
Prioritize safe wave clears and keep movement ready for boss attacks that punish standing still.
Treat survivability as part of your damage plan. Fewer knockdowns means more uptime against bosses.
This is the first Kingpin Trophy Shard node. Begin tracking shard progress toward the Kingpin Dethroned Trophy Accessory.
Upgrade underperforming Gear slots before pushing higher, especially Weapon for damage checks and Armor for survival.
Use this midpoint to refine your boss order habits: Capwolf pressure, Ratatoskr movement, Dracula Bloodstain control, and Kingpin spacing.
Squads should keep revive discipline and avoid overlapping risky plays during dense waves.

heroRewards: Squirrel Girl: Back Off, Bloodsucker! Emoji
Moon Knight: Blood Moon Knight Emoji
Jeff the Land Shark: Count Jeffula Emoji
items.focus: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare 40 is a major farming target because it combines account rewards, Kingpin Trophy Shards, and hero-specific emoji unlocks.
Keep improving your main hero instead of swapping too early. Character-specific progression matters for later clears.
At this point, weak Gear rolls should be scrapped into Uru Shards and redirected into the slots your build actually needs.
Boss consistency matters more than rushing. Avoid Dracula damage intake because Bloodstain stacks can turn into healing for him.
This is another key shard checkpoint. If you are farming the Trophy Accessory, repeat the highest Nightmare tier you can clear reliably.
Prepare for the Nightmare 70 jump by finishing core Traits and strengthening Arcana that supports damage, health, or critical output.

heroRewards: Jeff the Land Shark: Jeff the Night Shark Title
items.focus: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare 70 is the second major hero-specific target. It also opens the higher Arcana progression layer tied to Scroll of Blessing and Scroll of Midas.
Push here when your clears are stable. Shard rewards become the main reason to keep climbing beyond the title tier.
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare 100 is a prestige milestone. Build for consistent boss survival rather than only peak burst damage.
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare 120 is the top listed reward node. Use your strongest completed hero, optimized Gear, and upgraded Arcana before attempting repeat clears.

items.milestone: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Solo Clear Rule
items.focus: Solo attempts are much less forgiving because being KO'd ends the run immediately. In squad play, fallen heroes can revive after a short delay, so coordinated teams have more room to recover from mistakes.

items.milestone: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Shard Goal
items.focus: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, and 120 all contribute shards, so plan your farming route around the highest node you can clear consistently.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Difficulty Levels
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.subtitle: Compare Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Normal, Hard, Extreme, and Nightmare before choosing a run. Each tier changes the boss route, reward value, and progression demands.
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt starts with three standard difficulty levels and adds Nightmare as the endgame ladder after those tiers. Normal introduces Capwolf, Hard adds Ratatoskr, Extreme brings Dracula and hidden Kingpin objectives, and Nightmare runs the full boss gauntlet as a scaling challenge.

items.unlock: Entry difficulty for Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt.
items.bestFor: Learning wave flow, testing the six playable heroes, and getting your first Gear drops.
items.farmAdvice: Start here if your hero has weak Gear or no Trait investment. Move up once Capwolf feels consistent.

items.unlock: Progress after Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Normal.
items.bestFor: Practicing the second boss route and building enough Gear quality for Extreme.
items.farmAdvice: Use Hard when Normal is too easy but Extreme still feels unstable, especially if Ratatoskr is causing wipes.

bosses: Kingpin if hidden objectives are completed
items.unlock: Progress after Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Hard.
items.bestFor: Learning the full Dracula route, completing hidden Kingpin objectives, and preparing for Nightmare.
items.farmAdvice: Farm Extreme when you can clear Dracula safely. Complete the Phase 4, Phase 7, and Phase 12 hidden objectives if you want the Kingpin encounter and its first-time rewards.

items.unlock: Unlocks after beating the standard Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Normal, Hard, and Extreme path.
firstClearReward: Reward milestones begin at Nightmare 5 and continue through Nightmare 120
items.bestFor: Endgame farming, Kingpin Trophy Shards, hero-specific emojis, hero-specific titles, and solo-clear challenges.
items.farmAdvice: Only push the next Nightmare node when your current tier is reliable. For farming, repeat the highest Nightmare level you can clear without frequent deaths.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Gear Guide
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.subtitle: Understand Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Weapon, Armor, Accessory, and Exclusive Gear before spending Forge materials. Gear is the main loot layer that turns repeated runs into stronger clears.
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Every Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt hero has four Gear slots: Weapon, Armor, Accessory, and Exclusive. Gear drops from regular enemies and bosses, comes in five rarity tiers, and can be recycled into Uru Shards for targeted Forge attempts.

items.howToGet: Drops from regular enemies and bosses during Blood Hunt matches.
items.forgeUse: Craft this slot when boss damage feels too low or Dracula and Kingpin phases take too long.
items.nightmarePriority: Very high because faster boss clears reduce the number of dangerous mechanics you must survive.

items.howToGet: Drops from regular enemies and bosses during Blood Hunt matches.
items.forgeUse: Craft this slot when you are dying to wave pressure, boss burst, or failed dodges.
items.nightmarePriority: Very high for solo clears and late Nightmare levels where mistakes are heavily punished.

items.slotEffect: Adds bonus effects such as Critical Rate and Block Rate.
items.howToGet: Drops from regular enemies and bosses during Blood Hunt matches.
items.forgeUse: Craft this slot when your build needs a specific secondary stat to stabilize damage or defense.
items.nightmarePriority: High because Accessory bonuses can push a build from basic survival into consistent farming.

items.gearType: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Exclusive Gear
items.slotEffect: Character-specific Gear that strengthens a hero's abilities.
items.howToGet: Drops from regular enemies and bosses during Blood Hunt matches.
items.forgeUse: Craft this slot for your main hero when you want stronger ability scaling instead of generic stats.
items.nightmarePriority: High for hero mains because Exclusive Gear directly supports the character-specific playstyle you are investing in.

items.gearType: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Gear Rarity
items.slotEffect: Gear comes in five rarity tiers, and higher hero level improves the chance of stronger Gear quality.
items.howToGet: Keep playing and leveling the hero you want to build.
items.forgeUse: Use the Forge after leveling a hero to improve the odds of better crafted Gear.
items.nightmarePriority: Core progression: stronger rarity rolls are one of the cleanest ways to prepare for Nightmare pushes.

items.slotEffect: Recycles unwanted Gear into Uru Shards and crafts new Gear for a selected hero and slot.
items.howToGet: Open the Forge menu from Blood Hunt preparation systems.
items.forgeUse: Pick the hero and slot you need, then spend Uru Shards for a new Gear roll.
items.nightmarePriority: Essential when one weak slot is blocking progress or when you want to focus all resources on one main hero.

items.gearType: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Uru Shards
items.slotEffect: Crafting material created by scrapping or smelting Gear.
items.howToGet: Scrap unwanted Gear in the Forge menu.
items.forgeUse: Spend shards on targeted hero-and-slot crafting attempts.
items.nightmarePriority: Save shards for your main hero's weakest slots instead of spreading them across every character.

items.gearType: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Crafting Odds
items.slotEffect: Forge results involve RNG, and the in-game information icon shows the exact probabilities.
items.howToGet: Check the Forge screen before spending materials.
items.forgeUse: Use odds to decide whether to keep farming drops or spend Uru Shards now.
items.nightmarePriority: Important for efficient farming because wasted shards slow down late-game build completion.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Traits and Arcana
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.subtitle: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Traits and Arcana are the upgrade layers that make your hero scale beyond raw Gear. Use them to turn repeated runs into stronger Nightmare attempts.
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Traits are the hero-specific skill tree powered by Trait Points from matches and leveling. Arcana is the slower but heavier progression layer, using Arcana Points and scrolls to unlock larger stat boosts tied to damage, health, critical output, and higher difficulty milestones.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Trait Points
items.summary: Trait Points are earned by playing Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt matches and leveling up a hero.
items.content: Spend Trait Points in the Traits section for the hero you are actively building. Because progression is tied to hero investment, focusing one main character first usually creates faster Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare progress than spreading points evenly across all six heroes.
items.priority: Start here whenever your hero feels underpowered even with usable Gear.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt General Traits
items.summary: General Traits improve a hero's base attributes.
items.content: This branch is the safest early investment when you need more baseline stability. It supports every part of the run because better core stats help in waves, boss fights, and solo attempts.
items.priority: Take General Traits early if you are dying before boss phases or losing too much health during waves.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Signature Ability Traits
items.summary: Each hero also has two ability-focused Trait branches.
items.content: These branches improve the hero's signature damage tools. Blade, for example, has dedicated Trait paths for Purging Bladedance and Silver Soulbreaker, while The Punisher's branches improve his primary abilities.
items.priority: Invest here once you know which ability loop carries your build.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Arcana Points
items.summary: Arcana Points build more slowly than Trait Points but unlock stronger stat gains.
items.content: Arcana upgrades can increase important combat stats such as health, damage, and critical rate. This makes Arcana a major long-term layer for Nightmare, where small stat gaps become clear during boss gauntlets.
items.priority: Do not ignore Arcana just because it levels slower. It is one of the biggest late-game power sources.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Scroll of Conquest
items.summary: Unlocks from Normal difficulty and increases total output boost.
items.content: This scroll is the straightforward offensive Arcana path. Use it when your clears are safe but bosses are taking too long.
items.priority: Prioritize for damage-focused builds and higher Nightmare farming speed.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Scroll of Immortality
items.summary: Unlocks from Normal difficulty and increases health.
items.content: This scroll supports survival and gives your hero more room to recover from wave pressure or boss mistakes.
items.priority: Prioritize for solo clears, early Nightmare pushes, or any build that dies before finishing boss phases.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Scroll of Blessing
items.summary: Unlocks after clearing Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare 70.
items.content: This is one of the high-tier Arcana scrolls tied to late Nightmare progression. Treat Nightmare 70 as the gateway where Arcana progression expands beyond the basic Normal-unlocked scrolls.
items.priority: Target after your chosen hero can consistently reach late Nightmare milestones.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Scroll of Midas
items.summary: Unlocks after clearing Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare 70.
items.content: This is another high-tier Arcana scroll attached to the Nightmare 70 milestone. It gives long-term players an additional reason to keep improving one hero past the early reward nodes.
items.priority: Use it as part of the post-Nightmare 70 upgrade plan for deeper ladder pushing.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt In-Run Arcana Spending
items.summary: Players spend starting Arcana at the beginning of a run and can spend Arcana again between waves.
items.content: After waves, a rally point appears and the short downtime lets players spend Arcana before the next wave begins. Good Arcana choices help turn a difficult run into a stable boss attempt.
items.priority: Spend deliberately between waves instead of rushing forward with unused power.

items.label: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare Upgrade Route
items.summary: Use Gear, Traits, and Arcana together instead of treating them as separate systems.
items.content: A practical route is: fill basic Gear slots, invest General Traits for stability, add signature ability Traits for damage, scrap weak Gear into Uru Shards, then push Arcana upgrades as the Nightmare ladder demands more stats.
items.priority: Best for players stuck around Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Nightmare 40, Nightmare 70, or higher shard-farming nodes.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Bosses Guide
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.eyebrow: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Boss Guide
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.subtitle: Track Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt boss order from Capwolf to Kingpin with unlock difficulty, core mechanics, and fast strategy notes.
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt escalates from early vampire waves into a full boss gauntlet. Normal starts with Capwolf, Hard adds Ratatoskr, Extreme adds Dracula and the hidden Kingpin route, and Nightmare applies the full high-pressure ladder.

items.appearsFrom: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Normal and above
items.encounterRole: Opening boss and the first Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt durability check.
coreMechanics: Capwolf marks one player as Prey and prioritizes attacks on that target.
Capwolf uses shield and claw swipes, ground-smash leaps, forward charges, and a shield throw that can stun.
In phase two, Capwolf can heal by damaging the Prey and can channel a pin combo that should be interrupted with focused burst.
Near defeat, Capwolf gains regeneration and a large shield, so breaking the shield opens a punish window.
basicStrategy: Keep the marked Prey moving while the rest of the team attacks from safe angles.
Punish missed double pounces because Capwolf briefly stuns himself after both pounces miss.
Save burst cooldowns for the channel-pin and near-death shield windows.
items.bestForLearning: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt positioning, target focus, and interrupt timing.

items.appearsFrom: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Hard and above
items.encounterRole: Mobility and blood-zone pressure boss in Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt.
coreMechanics: Ratatoskr uses close-range bite and horn swings against players in front.
Ratatoskr leaps back to create distance, then charges forward and can restore health on hit.
Tail swings knock nearby players airborne and punish stacked positioning.
Enrage strengthens attacks and creates blood-stain zones, and critical damage shortens the enrage window.
basicStrategy: Stay out of the front cone and avoid fighting from tight corners.
Spread enough to limit blood-zone overlap, but stack inside split-damage impact circles when required.
Keep ranged pressure during sprint and leap cycles instead of chasing blindly.
items.bestForLearning: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt arena spacing, ranged uptime, and team damage sharing.

items.appearsFrom: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Extreme and Nightmare
items.encounterRole: Main Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt end boss.
coreMechanics: Players gain Bloodstain stacks after taking damage.
Dracula siphons Bloodstain stacks from all players and converts them into a large heal unless the channel is interrupted.
Dracula uses sword waves, frontal pull-and-shockwave attacks, tracking blood storms, and mist teleports that can become a damaging grab.
Final phases add sentient swords, persistent vampire spawns, and Norn Stone sunlight objective windows.
basicStrategy: Minimize damage before siphon channels so Dracula has fewer Bloodstain stacks to consume.
Interrupt siphon channels with focused burst and punish the stun window.
Destroy portal crystals quickly, enter the sunlight zone, and attack while empowered to trigger heavy vulnerability.
items.bestForLearning: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt stack management, burst interrupts, and phase objectives.

items.appearsFrom: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Extreme secret route and Nightmare
items.encounterRole: Hidden Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt final boss and event prize gate.
coreMechanics: Kingpin is unlocked in Extreme by completing hidden objectives before the post-Dracula secret phase.
Kingpin uses control effects that can stun or freeze players until they break out.
Charged Implosion creates a large danger zone that can instantly down exposed players.
Heavy jumps end in high AoE impact damage and punish slow repositioning.
basicStrategy: Enter Extreme with the team aligned on the required Phase 4, Phase 7, and Phase 12 objectives.
Maintain distance and sustained pressure instead of forcing close-range trades.
During Charged Implosion, either burst inside the dome fast enough to stun Kingpin or break line-of-sight behind map objects.
items.bestForLearning: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt secret-objective routing, cover usage, and burst-or-hide decisions.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Enemies and Waves
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.eyebrow: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Enemy Guide
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.subtitle: Use this Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt table to understand enemy categories, threat profiles, wave behavior, and counter plans.
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt waves combine melee vampires, ranged flask throwers, explosive rushers, flying enemies, and elite disruptors. Enemy strength rises by wave, cleared waves award Arcana opportunities, and the next wave begins when the team enters the rally point.

items.category: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Common Melee Swarmers
items.mainThreat: Fast close-range pressure that surrounds players and forces constant movement.
items.waveBehavior: These enemies pursue players directly. Savage Bloodfiends are faster and more aggressive, while Creeping Terrors attack from low angles and slow when repeatedly hit.
items.counterPlan: Use wide AoE, piercing projectiles, and constant kiting. Squirrel Girl, Moon Knight, and Jeff are strong wave-clear anchors.

items.category: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Ranged Bombers
items.mainThreat: Area denial from fire flasks and burning blood pools.
items.waveBehavior: Blight Bombers throw delayed explosive flasks while Blood-Blight Bombers leave persistent ground fire.
items.counterPlan: Do not fight inside lingering pools and prioritize bombers early when the team needs a safe kiting lane.

items.category: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Explosive Rushers
items.mainThreat: Large burst damage when it reaches players or is defeated nearby.
items.waveBehavior: Pustule Fiend carries a swollen blood sac and detonates on approach or on death.
items.counterPlan: Focus at range before it reaches the team and save mobility cooldowns for emergency spacing.

items.category: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Flying Enemies
items.mainThreat: Vertical pressure that punishes teams with weak ranged coverage.
items.waveBehavior: Sky Stalkers hover and dive, while Airborne Alchemists hold altitude and throw impact flasks.
items.counterPlan: Assign ranged heroes to clear flyers quickly. Hitting Sky Stalkers slows them and opens safer punish windows.

items.category: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Elite Bruiser
items.mainThreat: Tracking charges, knock-up pressure, and piercing shockwaves.
items.waveBehavior: Royal Bloodguard controls space with heavy melee chains and long shockwave lanes.
items.counterPlan: Keep lateral movement active, avoid straight retreats, and burst it down before it splits team positioning.

items.category: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Elite Support Caster
items.mainThreat: Enemy buffs, player slows, health drain, and teleport disengage.
items.waveBehavior: Darkhold Cultist amplifies nearby enemies and can teleport with temporary health after taking heavy damage.
items.counterPlan: Mark Darkhold Cultist as a priority target and collapse with burst before buffs and drains overwhelm the run.

items.category: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Elite Mobile Threats
items.mainThreat: Erratic movement, teleports, chained attacks, and pin-down health drain.
items.waveBehavior: Crazed Crawlers sprint and reposition with blood magic. Bloodwing Hunters teleport, dive, and pin teammates until interrupted.
items.counterPlan: Use tracking damage and fast target calls. When Bloodwing Hunter pins a teammate, focus it immediately to break the hold.

items.category: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Wave Progression Rules
items.mainThreat: Enemy density and pressure increase each wave while mission pacing affects Arcana economy.
items.waveBehavior: Clearing enemies and completing special wave missions awards Arcana. After each wave, entering the rally point starts the next wave early.
items.counterPlan: Spend Arcana during downtime, regroup before triggering rally points, and avoid pulling the next wave while teammates are still resetting.

items.category: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Defeat and Revive Rules
items.mainThreat: Solo KO ends the run immediately, and full team wipe ends the match if recovery fails.
items.waveBehavior: In teams, fallen heroes revive after a short delay. During defeat sequences, Lifeline Serum can return players to combat.
items.counterPlan: Solo players should value armor, health, mobility, and safe ranged uptime. Teams should keep at least one durable player alive for revive windows.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Solo and Co-op Tips
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.eyebrow: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Solo and Co-op
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.subtitle: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt supports one to four players and allows duplicate heroes, but role balance clears waves and bosses more consistently.
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: The safest Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt plan combines wave clear, boss burst, survivability, and revive stability. Solo players need self-sufficient heroes and clean kiting, while co-op teams should split responsibilities before pushing Extreme and Nightmare.

items.title: What should Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt solo players prioritize?
items.summary: Solo clears are possible, but a solo KO ends the attempt immediately, so survival must come first.
tips: Pick self-sufficient heroes with ranged pressure, mobility, or wide wave coverage.
Squirrel Girl is the most reliable all-round solo choice for both swarms and bosses.
Moon Knight is strong when built around Ankh-focused ranged uptime.
Jeff the Land Shark scales hard with Overflowing Waters and Forked Stream after investment.
Avoid stationary play against bombers, flyers, Kingpin jumps, Dracula pulls, and Bloodwing Hunter pins.

items.title: How should a Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt four-player team be built?
items.summary: Strong squads cover wave clear, boss burst, durability, and recovery instead of stacking one narrow job.
tips: Use Squirrel Girl or Moon Knight as the primary wave clearer.
Pair them with Jeff for high-scaling damage that hits groups and bosses.
Add Thor as a durable anchor who can survive while teammates revive.
Use The Punisher as a boss specialist only when the team already controls swarms.
Keep one flexible slot for the hero your team has invested most heavily.

items.title: Are duplicate heroes allowed in Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt?
items.summary: Duplicate heroes are allowed, so players do not need to contest the same pick.
tips: Duplicates help comfort picks and fast hero leveling with friends.
Stacking one hero is usually weaker than assigning distinct jobs across the squad.
Multiple Punishers can shred bosses but often struggle against dense vampire waves.
Multiple Blades need heavy investment to match safe ranged output from stronger early picks.
Thor and The Punisher usually provide more value as single-role specialists than stacked duplicates.

items.title: Which heroes are best for Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt wave clear?
items.summary: Wave clear depends on sustained AoE, safe ranged pressure, and mobility as wave density rises.
tips: Squirrel Girl handles dense vampire packs and single targets with minimal setup.
Moon Knight upgraded Ankhs provide crowd control and boss pressure in one build path.
Jeff can cover wide groups once Overflowing Waters and Forked Stream are unlocked.
Thor can hold space and buy time when waves become unstable.

items.title: Which heroes are best for Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt bosses?
items.summary: Boss fights reward safe ranged uptime, burst windows, and clean interrupt timing.
tips: Jeff becomes a major boss-damage option after enough gear and ability investment.
Moon Knight Ankh builds keep sustained pressure while retaining control utility.
The Punisher excels at single-target burst when the team already handles wave control.
Blade needs gun-focused setups for high-difficulty fights where melee uptime is risky.
Against Dracula, save burst for siphon interrupts and sunlight empowered windows.

items.title: How should Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt teams recover after mistakes?
items.summary: Co-op runs are forgiving only while at least one teammate survives to hold the run together.
tips: When teammates fall, the surviving player should kite safely instead of forcing damage.
Thor, Squirrel Girl, and Moon Knight are strong recovery anchors under pressure.
Use Lifeline Serum during defeat sequences when the team needs a manual return.
Do not trigger the next rally point before players spend Arcana and reset positioning.
Call out elite pins, Dracula channels, and Kingpin Charged Implosion immediately.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Patch Notes and Trailer
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.eyebrow: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Latest Update
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.subtitle: Follow official Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt patch notes, trailer updates, release windows, and key event milestones.
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt arrived through Version 20260423 as a Season 7.5 PvE event. The mode sends one to four players into Blood-Night New York to fight vampire hordes, climb difficulty tiers, and defeat Dracula before the event window closes.

items.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Version 20260423 Patch Notes Announcement
items.details: Official patch notes confirmed the April 23, 2026 update, introduced Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt as new PvE content, and outlined Dracula's escape setup for the New York vampire outbreak.
highlights: Patch scheduled for April 23, 2026 at 09:00 UTC.
Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt listed as new PvE mode content.

items.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt New PvE Mode Trailer
items.details: The official trailer presents Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt as a vampire-horde PvE mode with co-op combat, escalating difficulty, and Dracula as the central threat.
highlights: Shows squad-based PvE combat against Dracula's army.
Highlights difficulty progression and end-boss pressure.
Supports homepage video focus and latest-update context.

items.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Goes Live
items.details: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt launched as a limited-time Season 7.5 PvE event where one to four players progress through vampire waves, gear drops, Arcana upgrades, and boss checkpoints.
highlights: Hero roster includes Blade, Moon Knight, Jeff the Land Shark, Squirrel Girl, The Punisher, and Thor.
Players progress through Normal, Hard, Extreme, and Nightmare.

items.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Co-op Battle and Progression Coverage
items.details: Launch coverage summarized co-op structure, event hero limits, duplicate picks, Arcana scroll progression, gear growth, escalating enemy pressure, and major boss appearances.
highlights: Four-player co-op against AI vampire hordes.
Arcana and gear systems increase run power over time.
Enemy pressure includes mixed waves, elites, and boss transitions.

items.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Event Window Closes
items.details: The official Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt window runs from April 23, 2026 at 09:00 UTC to July 30, 2026 at 09:00 UTC, so reward completion and Nightmare progression are time-limited.
highlights: Complete difficulty clears before event close.
Finish hidden Kingpin routes and shard goals before expiration.

faq.title: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt

questions.question: Is Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt a standalone game?
questions.answer: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt is a limited-time PvE event mode inside Marvel Rivals, not a standalone game.

questions.question: When does Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt end?
questions.answer: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt is scheduled to end on July 30, 2026 at 09:00 UTC.

questions.question: How many players can join Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt?
questions.answer: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt supports solo play and 1-4 player co-op squads.

questions.question: What rewards are in Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt?
questions.answer: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt rewards include Units, Unstable Molecules, titles, moods, gallery cards, emoji, sprays, and Kingpin accessory shards.

questions.question: Which heroes are available in Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt?
questions.answer: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt features Blade, Jeff the Land Shark, Moon Knight, The Punisher, Squirrel Girl, and Thor.

questions.question: How do I unlock the Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Kingpin route?
questions.answer: Marvel Rivals Blood Hunt Kingpin route opens by completing the hidden objective chain across required event phases.
